title: Afghanistan: The Soviet War
pages_or_sections: The Resistance Fronts, esp. pp. 81-87 equivalent; lines 3558-3565, 3748-3764, 3926-3958
strengths: Original field-reporting source with explicit population, troop-strength, and casualty wording; strongest single source for a campaign-level Panjshir 1982 estimate.
limitations: Not a named-victim registry; some figures are attributed to resistance and diplomatic sources; the key death totals are campaign-level and not cleanly separable into May-only losses.
notes: Core source for the completed episode.

title: The MacNeil/Lehrer Report; Afghanistan -- War Continues
author_or_org: American Archive of Public Broadcasting / Bill Dowell / Edward Girardet
strengths: Contemporaneous eyewitness reporting with concrete geographic details (70-mile valley) and direct observations on sheltering, duration, and troop ratio.
limitations: Casualty statements are mostly qualitative; not a formal death-count source; reflects the early phase of the 1982 fighting rather than full-campaign totals.
notes: Important corrective against over-attributing full-1982 campaign casualty totals to the May offensive alone.

title: The Soviet Afghan War
strengths: Clear, compact secondary synthesis that directly cites Edward Girardet and offers a usable combined-force range.
limitations: Appears to compress broader 1982 campaign casualty figures into a month-long May-June operation; should not be used uncritically for episode boundaries.
notes: Used for triangulation and force-size range, not as the sole casualty anchor.

title: The Soviet-Afghan War: How a Superpower Fought and Lost
author_or_org: Russian General Staff; translated/edited by Lester W. Grau and Michael A. Gress
strengths: Explicit operational phases, date of attack, and scale of heliborne insertion.
limitations: Attacker-centric and not a mortality audit; force numbers are tactical context rather than denominator-ready monthly exposures.
notes: Used mainly for timing, theater, and combatant concentration context.

title: The Soviet-Afghan War: How a Superpower Fought and Lost
author_or_org: Russian General Staff; translated/edited by Lester W. Grau and Michael A. Gress
pages_or_sections: Phases and Course of the Conflict; lines 1571-1576 and 2125-2133
strengths: Explicit phase framing of the later war and direct mention of Operation Magistral as a distinct exception during a generally less offensive Soviet phase.
limitations: Not a casualty source; phase scheme reflects Soviet doctrinal framing.
notes: Used to justify splitting out the late-war Paktia/Khost corridor episode.

title: The Muslim Battalions: Soviet Central Asians in the Soviet-Afghan War
strengths: Useful secondary confirmation of the May and September force packages and of the wider 1982 Panjshir campaign framing.
limitations: Casualty wording mixes dead and wounded/casualties; not clean enough for core direct-death ingestion without transformation.
notes: Used as a corroborating but non-core mortality source.

title: One of the fiercest battles since Soviet troops invaded...
source_type: journalistic report (search-result snippet; full fetch blocked)
pages_or_sections: search-result snippet; full article inaccessible via fetch
strengths: Contemporaneous direct-death wording for Soviet/Afghan government-force victims.
limitations: Snippet only; sourcing chain and exact scope of the underlying claim cannot be fully audited from the blocked article.
notes: Used cautiously as the cleanest available direct-death anchor for Soviet/DRA military victims.

title: War Uproots Afghan Valley's Villagers
geographic_scope: Panjshir Valley and neighboring refuge zones
strengths: Clear evacuation and death claims for the 1984 Panjshir episode.
limitations: Civilian and guerrilla death figures are explicitly from rebel sources; geographic spillover beyond the valley complicates denominator design.
notes: Good candidate for a future episode estimate, but not complete enough yet.

title: Afghanistan: The Forgotten War — Human Rights Abuses and Violations of the Laws of War Since the Soviet Withdrawal
strengths: Explicit late-war civilian death claim tied to a specific withdrawal-period bombing episode.
limitations: Uses reported-claim language; no combatant or exposure series.
notes: Useful for episode mapping and future civilian-only or censored DR work.

title: Afghanistan: Internal and External Situation
strengths: Useful national context on overall Soviet and Afghan government force levels around early 1982.
limitations: National-level only and therefore inappropriate as a direct subnational denominator.
notes: Retained only as ceiling/context; not used as the Panjshir denominator.
